title: s "Full Automation" in trading a myth, or are we just doing it wrong?
author: Upbeat_Cabinet_9628
selftext: It feels like the conversation around AI trading is shifting. It’s no longer just about "automating everything" with a simple script. The real challenge is building systems that can actually stay active and adapt when the market gets messy. I’ve been looking into this lately, and it seems like the next phase is all about integrating multiple data sources to handle changing conditions in real-time. I noticed that platforms like **MOTA** are starting to reflect this exact direction—focusing on continuous operation rather than just flashy signals. Do you guys think we’re finally moving past simplistic bots, or is the "set and forget" dream still dead?
